display.purpose: Page titles, wordmark, influencer names on detail header.

body.purpose: Stat labels, table content, recommendation copy, nav links.

shadows.name: card-ambient
shadows.purpose: The system's single elevation level, applied to .card.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Solid Signal Blue Deep submit/primary action button (login, forms). Gradient fill retired for AA contrast.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #1c6fc4; color: #ffffff; font-weight: 600; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; border: none; transition: opacity 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #2e8ff0; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; }

components.name: Ghost Nav Link
components.description: Nav bar link/action, muted at rest, ink on hover.
components.css: .ds-nav-link { color: #6b7684; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-nav-link:hover, .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { color: #12181f; }

components.name: Card
components.description: The single surface primitive used for roster cards, forms, and detail panels.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e1e7ed; border-radius: 1.125rem; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(20,30,45,0.04), 0 8px 24px -12px rgba(20,30,45,0.12); padding: 1.5rem; }

components.name: Text Input
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; background: #eef3f6; border: 1px solid #e1e7ed; border-radius: 0.5rem; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; color: #12181f; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.15s ease-out; } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: #2e8ff0; }

components.name: Wordmark
components.description: Brand lockup: 'You First' in Ink, 'Gersh' in solid Signal Blue. No gradient text.
components.css: .ds-wordmark { font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: #12181f; } .ds-wordmark-accent { color: #2e8ff0; }

narrative.overview: A calm, high-signal instrument for people making real decisions about real careers. Talent managers scan dozens of influencers a day; the system's job is to make "who needs attention" answerable at a glance, then get out of the way. Density is embraced, but never at the cost of scanability — every screen is a triage screen first, a report second. This system explicitly rejects generic AI-dashboard scaffolding: gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, hero-metric-template stat blocks, and identical icon-card grids that could belong to any SaaS product. Restraint is deliberate: the blue/teal accent pair is a signal, not decoration, and its rarity is what keeps it meaningful.
keyCharacteristics: Cool, near-white canvas with a single card surface — no visual competition with the data.
One accent hue pair (blue primary, teal secondary) used sparingly and consistently as the signal color.
Semantic color (positive/negative) reserved strictly for trend direction, never decoration.
Plus Jakarta Sans for display weight, Inter for everything read at length.

rules.name: The Signal Rule
rules.body: Signal Blue and Signal Teal together account for a small minority of any screen's surface. If the accent pair is more than a fifth of what's visible, something has been decorated instead of designed.

rules.name: The Solid Text Rule
rules.body: Text is always a solid color from the palette. Gradients are reserved for fills (buttons, subtle chart accents) and never applied to text via background-clip: text.

rules.name: The One Shadow Rule
rules.body: There is exactly one elevation level. Introducing a second (e.g. a heavier modal shadow) requires a deliberate reason, not a hover-state reflex.

dos: Do keep Signal Blue and Signal Teal rare and intentional — the Signal Rule.
Do use Signal Blue Deep (#1c6fc4) for any fill that carries white text; plain Signal Blue and the teal→blue gradient both fail AA under white text.
Do use the single Card Ambient shadow for every elevated surface; don't invent a second shadow scale.
Do reserve Positive/Negative color strictly for trend direction and error states.
donts: Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text + gradient). This was present on the wordmark and has been replaced with solid Signal Blue.
Don't add tiny uppercase tracked eyebrows above sections — not part of this system's vocabulary.
Don't build identical icon+heading+text card grids; roster and detail layouts are data-first, not marketing-card-first.
Don't use glassmorphism or decorative blur — the system is flat and opaque.
Don't reach for the hero-metric-template (big number + small label + gradient accent) for stat tiles; let the number and its trend delta carry the weight without extra chrome.
Don't introduce a warm-tinted neutral background; Canvas stays cool (blue-gray), matching the Trading Floor register.
